module.name: Dosimetry & Exposures
module.tagline: Tracking of ionizing radiation and physical agent exposures

parameters.title: Dosimetry settings
parameters.subtitle: Regulatory limits, exposure bands and access policy — ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3 compliant

thresholds.empty: No threshold configured. Click 'Add threshold' or reset to ICRP defaults.

thresholdsPage.title: Thresholds & overexposures
thresholdsPage.subtitle: Configurable thresholds per mine, person category and dosimetric quantity. ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3 reference — editable if national legislation is stricter.

actions.resetTooltip: Restores reference values from V005 seed (requires DOSIMETRY_ADMIN)

table.doubleClickHint: Double-click a value to edit it inline
table.empty: No threshold matches the filters. Widen the selection or reset filters.

modal.addSubtitle: Creates a threshold scoped to a mine, a category and a quantity. If any value diverges by more than 50% from the ICRP reference, confirmation will be required.
modal.justificationPlaceholder: Explain why this derogation is needed (stricter national law, operational context, RPO recommendation, etc.).

errors.justificationMin: Justification must be at least 30 characters
errors.monotonic: Values must be ascending: constraint ≤ investigation ≤ action ≤ limit

deviation.title: Significant deviation from ICRP reference
deviation.body: The value entered for {{field}} ({{value}} mSv) diverges by more than 50% from the ICRP reference ({{reference}} mSv) for this category. Confirm save?

modal.saveError: Could not save threshold. Please check the inputs.

modal.title: Alert details
modal.noteAckPlaceholder: Describe the acknowledgement reason (analysis, immediate action taken…).
modal.noteResolvePlaceholder: Describe the resolution (corrective actions, return to normal…).
modal.openCaseSoon: Overexposure case opening will be available in Phase 6.

workerForm.subtitle: Nominative record of a worker exposed to ionizing radiation — ICRP 103 classification, exposure profile, special status and qualifications.

validation.reasonMin: Reason must contain at least 50 characters
validation.exposureTypesRequired: At least one exposure type is required
validation.workAreasRequired: At least one work area is required
validation.apprenticeBirthRequired: Birth date is required for apprentices
validation.certifyRequired: You must certify the accuracy of the data
validation.completeBeforeNext: Please complete required fields before continuing.
validation.completeBeforeSubmit: Please complete required fields before submitting.

identity.employeeTooltip: Select the relevant employee. HR information (ID, position, department) will be auto-filled.

classification.categoryTooltip: Category A: exposure likely to exceed 6 mSv/year (limit 20). Category B: exposure ≤ 6 mSv/year. Reference: IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106.
classification.reasonTooltip: Detailed justification: type of exposure, expected level, locations and frequency. Minimum 50 characters.
classification.reasonPlaceholder: Describe the conditions and justification of the classification (locations, equipment, frequency, expected dose)…
classification.rpoTooltip: Designated radiation protection officer. Required for regulatory traceability.

exposure.typesTooltip: List of relevant exposure types for this position. Phase 1: external radiation only.
exposure.workAreasTooltip: Areas where the worker operates (controlled, supervised, etc.).
exposure.positionsHint: Enter positions freely. Press Enter to confirm.
exposure.frequencyPlaceholder: Describe the frequency (daily, weekly…), average duration, protective equipment worn…

special.statusTooltip: Triggers a tightened regulatory limit for pregnancy (1 mSv for the fetus) or apprentices 16-18 (6 mSv/yr).
special.pregnancyBannerText: Per ICRP 103 §187, the equivalent dose to the fetus must not exceed 1 mSv during pregnancy. Reassess the position and consider a temporary reclassification.
special.pregnancyNotePlaceholder: Notes on position adjustment, risk assessment, etc.
special.apprenticeBannerText: Per IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.108, the annual limit for apprentices and students aged 16 to 18 is 6 mSv/yr (instead of 20 mSv/yr).
special.crossWarningText: Category A combined with declared pregnancy: immediate reclassification is required. The RPO and occupational physician must be notified.

qualifications.itemsTooltip: List of obtained qualifications: RPO, industrial radiography, sealed sources, etc.
qualifications.certifyLabel: I certify that the data entered is accurate and compliant with the internal regulations and ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3 requirements.

help.title: Help & regulatory references

steps.0: Select the existing employee from the HR directory. ID, position and department will be auto-filled.
steps.1: A/B classification is an ICRP 103 regulatory obligation. The justification must be detailed enough (≥ 50 characters) to be defensible during an ASN/IAEA audit.
steps.2: Describe the actual exposure profile: radiation types, areas visited and exposure conditions.
steps.3: Special status triggers tightened limits: 1 mSv for the fetus (pregnancy), 6 mSv/yr for apprentices 16-18.
steps.4: Review the summary and check the certification box before validation.

help.procedureText: Category A applies to workers likely to receive an annual dose above 6 mSv or eye lens equivalent dose > 15 mSv. Otherwise, Category B. The RPO must validate any classification.
help.privacyText: Sensitive personal data (GDPR art. 9). 50-year retention after end of activity (IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106). Every access is logged in the SafeX 360 audit trail.

assign.title: Assign a dosimeter
assign.subtitle: Formalize the handover of a dosimeter to an exposed worker — receipt acknowledgement tracked.

return.title: Return a dosimeter
return.subtitle: Record the return of a dosimeter and the observed device condition.
return.noAssignment: No assignment loaded. Check the assignmentId parameter.
return.conditionNotePlaceholder: Describe the observed state (scratches, impact, malfunction, etc.)
return.photoPlaceholderHint: Photo upload will be available in Phase 4 — please provide a written description for now.
return.ackLabel: I confirm the return of the dosimeter and the declared condition

dosimeter.selectPlaceholder: Search by serial number or QR…
dosimeter.selectTooltip: Only dosimeters with status AVAILABLE are shown. You can also scan a QR code.

worker.selectPlaceholder: Search by employee ID or name…
worker.reinforcedBody: This worker is classified as Category A without special status: enhanced medical surveillance applies per ICRP 103 and labour code.

handover.notePlaceholder: Device state at handover (battery, calibration, accessories…)
handover.ackLabel: I confirm the handover to the worker and the traceability of this acknowledgement

scanner.title: Scan a QR code
scanner.hint: Enter or paste the dosimeter QR value (camera interface coming in Phase 4).
scanner.notFound: No available dosimeter matches this QR.

validation.periodOrder: End date must be after start date
validation.completeBeforeSubmit: Complete all required fields before submitting.

doseEntryForm.subtitleNew: Append-only entry for a monthly dosimetric measurement — compliant with ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3. Hp(10), Hp(0.07) and Hp(3) values are checked against regulatory limits.
doseEntryForm.subtitleEdit: Correction of an existing record. In accordance with IAEA requirements, the original is preserved and a new version is created.
doseEntryForm.editWarningBody: This edit will create a NEW version. The original record will be marked as superseded but kept in database for IAEA traceability. Provide the correction reason in the dedicated field.

measurements.belowDetectionTooltip: Check if the dosimeter returned values below the detection threshold. Hp(10), Hp(0.07) and Hp(3) values will be forced to 0 and the belowDetection flag will be set.

hp10.title: Hp(10) — whole-body effective dose
hp10.description: 10 mm depth — effective dose used for ICRP 103 annual limits.
hp10.tooltip: Hp(10) is the personal dose equivalent at 10 mm depth in soft tissue. It is the reference quantity to estimate the effective dose per ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3 for whole-body external exposures.
hp10.highWarning: High value (> {{threshold}} mSv) — requires RPO validation before final recording.

hp007.title: Hp(0.07) — skin / extremities dose
hp007.description: 0.07 mm depth — equivalent dose to skin and extremities.
hp007.tooltip: Hp(0.07) is the personal dose equivalent at 0.07 mm, used for skin and extremity exposures (hands, forearms). ICRP 103 annual limit: 500 mSv Category A.

hp3.title: Hp(3) — eye lens dose
hp3.description: 3 mm depth — equivalent dose to the eye lens.
hp3.tooltip: Hp(3) is the personal dose equivalent at 3 mm, dedicated to lens exposure assessment. ICRP 103 annual limit: 20 mSv Category A (revised since 2011).
hp3.highWarning: High value (> {{threshold}} mSv) — risk of lens opacification, RPO validation required.

sourceTooltips.AGENCY: Official reading performed by an approved agency (passive dosimetry lab: TLD, OSL, film). Regulatory enforceable value.
sourceTooltips.EPD: Real-time reading from an electronic operational dosimeter. Useful for daily monitoring, complementary to agency reading.
sourceTooltips.ESTIMATED: Reconstructed estimate (dosimeter loss, unplanned exposure, retrospective calculation). Must be justified in notes.

attachments.uploadHint: Agency report upload will be enabled in Phase 4 Frontend-B.
attachments.supersedeReasonPlaceholder: Describe precisely why this correction is required (entry error, new agency reading, etc.).

cumulative.selectWorkerHint: Select a worker first to visualise their live cumulative doses.
cumulative.investigationBannerBody: This entry would cross the Hp(10) investigation level ({{level}} mSv/year). A radiation protection investigation must be triggered by the RPO.
cumulative.exceededBannerBody: Projected annual cumulative exceeds the regulatory limit of {{limit}} mSv (Category A). This dose must be validated by the RPO and reported to the safety authority.

submit.highValueBody: One or more values exceed internal warning thresholds. An additional confirmation will be requested before recording.

modal.title: Confirmation required — high values
modal.body: You are about to record an abnormally high dose. This value will be flagged as requiring RPO (Radiation Protection Officer) validation. Do you confirm the accuracy of the entered data?

validation.periodOrder: End date must be on or after start date
validation.supersedeReasonRequired: Provide a correction reason (10 chars minimum)
validation.completeBeforeSubmit: Complete all required fields before submission.

success.versionCreated: New dose version created. Original is preserved for traceability.

errors.loadFailed: Unable to load the dose to edit.
errors.submitFailed: Recording failed. Check the entered data.

help.title: Dose entry help
help.readingBody: The agency reading report typically provides the three quantities Hp(10), Hp(0.07) and Hp(3). Enter these values as-is in the corresponding fields, in mSv. If the value is below the detection threshold (often 0.01 or 0.02 mSv), check the 'below detection threshold' checkbox which will force values to 0.
help.limitsRef: Reference: ICRP Publication 103 (2007), IAEA GSR Part 3 (2014). Cat B limits are 3/10 of Cat A limits for Hp(10).

procedureSteps.0: Verify the measurement (re-reading, second dosimeter, EPD) to confirm.
procedureSteps.1: Immediately notify the RPO and the competent occupational physician.
procedureSteps.2: Withdraw the worker from controlled areas pending investigation.
procedureSteps.3: Open a radiation protection investigation (causes, circumstances, duration).
procedureSteps.4: Report to the nuclear safety authority within regulatory deadlines.

doseTracking.loadError: Unable to load dose tracking for this worker.

alerts.bannerSubtitle: Action required — review and acknowledge the alerts below.
alerts.acknowledgeDenied: Permission required to acknowledge an alert

table.title: Dose history
table.subtitle: Active records (not superseded) sorted by period descending.
table.empty: No active record for this worker.

trend.title: Hp(10) trend vs annual limit
trend.subtitle: Chronological evolution and annual cumulation (resets each civil year).
trend.empty: Not enough data to draw the trend.

footer.title: Confidential data — Access logged
footer.note: Every consultation is logged. Tracking compliant with ICRP 103, IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106 and GDPR art. 30.

csvImportWizard.title: Dose CSV import
csvImportWizard.subtitle: Import a batch of monthly dosimetric records from your dosimetry provider. Upfront validation, dry-run and audit-logged import compliant with IAEA / GDPR.

actions.exportHelp: How to export from your dosimetry provider

select.dropSubtitle: or click to browse — UTF-8, max 10 MB
select.formatHint: First row = headers. UTF-8 encoding (with or without BOM). Separator: comma.

fields.matricule: Exposed worker code (must exist in the registry).
fields.hp10: Hp(10) in mSv — whole body. Decimal, dot separator.
fields.attachment_url: URL of a stored attachment (provider report).

select.rejectedBody: The file must be a valid .csv under 10 MB.
select.noMineWarning: No mine selected. Please pick a mine before importing.

preview.loadingBody: Parsing the structure and running consistency checks.
preview.noRows: No row could be read from the file.
preview.allErrors: All rows are in error — please fix the file before proceeding.
preview.someErrors: Some rows are in error — they will be skipped if you continue.

options.skipDuplicatesHint: If an active DoseRecord already exists for the worker and period, the row will be skipped.
options.continueOnWarningsHint: Includes flagged rows (high values, near duplicates) even with warnings.
options.recapBody: You are about to import {{count}} dose record(s) for the selected mine.
options.recapAlerts: Exposure alerts may be triggered automatically if thresholds are exceeded.
options.auditNote: This import will be journaled in DosimetryAuditLog (action=IMPORT) for IAEA / GDPR traceability.

result.successBody: Records have been created in append-only mode and exposure alerts generated when needed.
result.noErrors: No row remained in error — well done!
result.failureBody: A server-side error occurred. Check the file and try again.

help.title: Help & reference
help.formatBody: The file must be UTF-8 encoded and start with a header row. Decimal values use the dot. Periods follow the YYYY-MM format.

errorCodes.WORKER_NOT_FOUND: Matricule not found in the exposed worker registry.
errorCodes.INVALID_FORMAT: Invalid row format (missing columns or wrong type).
errorCodes.INVALID_DOSE: Invalid dose value (must be a number ≥ 0).
errorCodes.DUPLICATE: An active DoseRecord already exists for this worker and period.
errorCodes.NEAR_DUPLICATE: Near duplicate detected (same measurements within 7 days).
errorCodes.HIGH_VALUE: High value — please verify before importing.

help.privacyBody: Imported data is nominative and confidential. Every import is logged (who, when, how many rows). Access is restricted to bearers of DOSIMETRY_WRITE / DOSIMETRY_READ_NOMINATIVE. You are responsible for the purpose and legal basis of this import.

errors.previewFailed: Preview failed. Check the file and try again.
errors.noFile: Please select a CSV file first.
errors.mineRequired: No mine selected for the import.

overexposureCases.title: Overexposure cases
overexposureCases.subtitle: Manage overexposure investigation files — OPEN / INVESTIGATING / CLOSED workflow aligned with ICRP 103 and IAEA GSR Part 3.

actions.downloadReportHint: Generates a detailed PDF report of the case (cause, corrective actions, medical decision). GDPR reason required.

investigation.title: Investigation & corrective actions
investigation.subtitle: Root cause, corrective measures and medical decision.
investigation.modalSubtitle: Document the root cause, corrective actions and associated medical decision.

medicalSection.title: Medical decision (confidential)
medicalSection.subtitle: Access restricted to DOSIMETRY_MEDICAL permission holders.
medicalSection.denied: You do not have DOSIMETRY_MEDICAL — the medical decision is hidden.
medicalSection.empty: No medical decision recorded for this case.

authority.title: Authority declaration
authority.subtitle: If the overexposure exceeds the regulatory limit, declaration to the competent authority is mandatory.
authority.checkboxLabel: Tick if the declaration to the authority is filed

closure.title: Case closure
closure.subtitle: Closure is final and requires DOSIMETRY_PCR_RPO.
closure.closedBanner: This case has been closed since {{date}}.
closure.modalSubtitle: This action is irreversible. The authority declaration must be confirmed if applicable.
closure.notePlaceholder: Describe the final resolution, lessons learned…
closure.authorityConfirm: I confirm the declaration to the competent authority is filed (or not required).

audit.title: Audit trail
audit.subtitle: All events for this case — append-only, GDPR art.30 compliant.
audit.empty: No audit trace for this case.

form.subtitle: Open an official investigation file following a regulatory overexposure.

alert.title: Source alert selection
alert.subtitle: Link the case to an EXCEEDED alert not yet processed, or open a manual case.
alert.workerHelp: Pre-filled from the alert if selected.
alert.levelHelp: Auto-filled from the alert. For manual opening, choose the reached level.

characterization.title: Overexposure characterization
characterization.subtitle: Document the initial cause and immediate conservatory measures.
characterization.causePlaceholder: Describe the initial cause (e.g. EPD malfunction, prolonged exposure…)
characterization.conservatoryPlaceholder: Describe immediate measures taken (removal from station, medical follow-up…)

confirmation.title: Summary & confirmation
confirmation.subtitle: Check the information before officially opening the case.
confirmation.notifyBody: On opening, the PCR/RPO and the occupational physician will be notified automatically.
confirmation.confirmCheckbox: I confirm the official opening of the overexposure case.
confirmation.confirmRequired: You must confirm the opening to submit.

nav.completeBeforeNext: Please complete required fields before continuing.

form.notifyLogMedical: [Simulated notification] Occupational physician notified for case #{{id}}.

points.title: Ambient measurement points
points.subtitle: Registry of fixed H*(10) monitoring points — continuous oversight of surveilled and controlled areas (ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3).
points.loadError: Unable to load measurement points. Please retry later.

toolbar.searchPlaceholder: Search by code, label or location…

empty.title: No measurement points configured
empty.subtitle: Define fixed H*(10) monitoring points to start tracking your mine's regulated areas.

footer.title: Compliant ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3
footer.note: Ambient monitoring relies on regular H*(10) readings in µSv/h. The per-point reference level triggers exceedance alerts.

form.subtitle: Configure a fixed H*(10) monitoring point — unique per-mine code, zone classification and reference level (uSv/h).

fields.label: Label
fields.labelDesc: Short description of the point (e.g. North gallery entrance).
fields.zoneDesc: Optional — link the point to an existing work area.
fields.zoneClassificationDesc: ICRP 103 — Surveilled (>1 mSv/yr) / Controlled (>6 mSv/yr) / Unclassified.
fields.referenceLevelDesc: Alert threshold on the measured dose rate.
fields.referenceLevelHint: The reference level triggers an alert when the H*(10) reading exceeds it. Must be > 0.

errors.codeAlreadyUsed: This code is already used in this mine.
errors.referenceLevelNumber: Reference level must be a number.
errors.referenceLevelPositive: Reference level must be strictly positive.
errors.invalid: Please fix the form errors before submitting.

detail.loadError: Unable to load the measurement point.

chart.title: 6-month trend
chart.subtitle: Monthly aggregate — average and maximum.
chart.empty: Not enough data to plot a trend.

table.title: Measurement history
table.subtitle: Last {{count}} measurements
table.empty: No measurements recorded for this point.

footer.title: Regulatory traceability
footer.note: Ambient measurements are append-only and logged for audit (IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106).

map.title: Ambient H*(10) map
map.subtitle: Map view of measurement points coloured by the last dose rate (µSv/h).
map.loadError: Unable to load the ambient map.

toolbar.searchAria: Search a point on the map

empty.title: No point to display
empty.subtitle: Adjust filters or configure measurement points to render the ambient map.

hint.grid: Grid view — add geographic coordinates to enable the map layout

footer.title: Decision-support visualization
footer.note: This visualization aggregates the last measurements per point over the selected period. For a full 3D map and advanced clustering, plan a build with a Mapbox / Leaflet library.

fields.pointDesc: Select the fixed point this measurement relates to.
fields.instrumentDesc: Pick from the instrument / dosimeter inventory.
fields.instrumentSerialDesc: Free text when the instrument is not in the inventory.

errors.valuePositive: Measured value must be strictly positive.

overConfirm.title: Reference level exceeded
overConfirm.message: The entered value ({{value}} µSv/h) exceeds the point reference level ({{reference}} µSv/h).
overConfirm.question: Confirm recording this measurement? An ambient alert will be raised.

list.title: Ambient monitoring campaigns
list.subtitle: Plan, run and close H*(10) measurement campaigns covering a set of surveilled and controlled areas.
list.loadError: Unable to load campaigns. Please retry later.

toolbar.searchPlaceholder: Search by code, label or owner…

empty.title: No campaigns yet
empty.subtitle: Launch your first campaign to structure H*(10) readings by period and scope.

footer.title: ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3 compliant tracking
footer.note: Campaigns formalise the planning of ambient measurements, regulatory traceability and periodic reporting.

form.subtitle: Define the scope, protocol and owner. The campaign is created as a draft and started explicitly.
form.successStart: Campaign started — status moved to Ongoing.

fields.label: Label
fields.objectiveDesc: Describe the regulatory or operational purpose.
fields.responsibleDesc: Employee in charge of running the campaign.
fields.protocolPlaceholder: Weekly readings with SURVEY_METER #X, rotating through covered points…
fields.measurementPointsDesc: Pick the fixed points included in the campaign scope.

errors.codeAlreadyUsed: This code is already used in this mine.
errors.endBeforeStart: End date cannot precede start date.
errors.invalid: Please fix the form errors before continuing.

warnings.noPointsSelected: No point selected — you can add them later from the campaign detail.

createdAlert.title: Campaign created as draft
createdAlert.message: Start the campaign to move it to Ongoing, or open the detail to fine-tune the scope.

points.title: Covered points ({{count}})
points.empty: No point covered — edit the campaign to add measurement points.

measurements.title: Recorded measurements ({{count}})
measurements.empty: No measurement recorded for this campaign.

report.title: Campaign report
report.description: The report aggregates the recorded measurements, per-point statistics and any observed exceedances. This first version ships as plain text (PDF placeholder).
report.empty: No report generated — click "Generate report" to produce the text summary.

audit.title: Audit log ({{count}})
audit.empty: No event recorded for this campaign.

start.title: Start campaign
start.message: You are about to start the campaign. Its status will move to Ongoing and recorded measurements will count.

complete.title: Complete campaign
complete.message: You are about to close the campaign. No further measurement can be linked to it afterwards.

cancel.title: Cancel campaign
cancel.message: You are about to cancel the campaign. This action is traced but cannot be reverted.

footer.title: Traceability & compliance
footer.note: Campaigns and their measurements are append-only and audited (IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106).

list.title: Worker exposure profiles
list.subtitle: Link each profile to ambient measurement points with a time-presence fraction, and estimate the corresponding annual dose.
list.loadError: Cannot load exposure profiles. Please try again later.

toolbar.searchPlaceholder: Search a profile, worker or point code…

empty.title: No exposure profile registered
empty.subtitle: Create exposure profiles from the worker view to be able to link them to ambient measurement points.

footer.title: Indicative estimate — ICRP 103
footer.note: Annual estimates rely on the mean of the available ambient measurements per point. For regulatory individual tracking, use the individual dose records.

editor.noLinks: No link defined yet for this profile. Click on "Add point" to start.

points.title: Measurement points and time fractions
points.subtitle: Add the points the worker visits and provide the fraction of time spent on each. The sum must remain ≤ 1.

estimate.title: Estimated annual dose calculation
estimate.subtitle: The estimate relies on the mean of the latest ambient measurements available for each point.

errors.invalidProfile: Exposure profile not found or invalid id.
errors.saveFailed: Cannot save links. Please try again later.
errors.fractionOverflow: Sum of fractions exceeds 1.0 — adjust the values before saving.
errors.incompleteLine: Each row must have a measurement point selected.

notice.title: Indicative estimate
notice.body: Estimate based on the latest ambient measurements available. For an actual individual measurement, use individual dose records (Phase 4).

medical.rgpdBanner: Audited access — GDPR Article 30 + IAEA GSR Part 3. Every clinical-data view is recorded nominally.

planning.title: Medical visits planning
planning.subtitle: Regulatory visit pilot board (IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.105), alerts for expiring fitness assessments and temporary unfit cases.
planning.loadError: Unable to load scheduled visits. Please try again later.
planning.rbacBlockedBody: This page is reserved to occupational physicians and RPO/PCR (DOSIMETRY_MEDICAL or DOSIMETRY_PCR_RPO).

table.empty: No visits scheduled in the selected period.

visit.scheduleSubtitle: Convoke an exposed worker for a regulatory visit.
visit.rbacBlockedBody: Only the occupational physician (DOSIMETRY_MEDICAL) can record a visit.
visit.encryptionBody: This data will be encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM and accessible only to the occupational physician with an audited justification.
visit.lockedBody: The visit has been recorded and locked. You can now issue the related fitness assessment.
visit.step3Help: Click on « Enter fitness assessment » to issue and sign the related fitness assessment.

fields.generalConclusionPlaceholder: E.g. « Visit performed, fitness issued separately »
fields.generalConclusionDescription: This text remains visible to RPO/PCR and exposes no clinical data.
fields.detailedReportPlaceholder: Confidential report — encrypted AES-256-GCM at rest…

visit.confirmBody: You are about to lock this visit. Once recorded, the visit becomes append-only (IAEA §3.106) and can no longer be modified.

fitness.title: New fitness assessment
fitness.subtitle: Regulatory medical assessment (IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.105) — append-only after signature.
fitness.rbacBlockedBody: Only the occupational physician (DOSIMETRY_MEDICAL) can issue a fitness card.
fitness.appendOnlyBody: After signature, this document becomes append-only (IAEA §3.106) and archived for 30 years. Any correction will require a new complementary card.
fitness.confirmBody: This card will be digitally signed and archived. You will not be able to modify it — only a new card can update it.

fields.restrictionsPlaceholder: Detailed medical description of restrictions (forbidden zones, contra-indications, etc.)…
fields.restrictionsDescription: AES-256-GCM encrypted — accessible only to the occupational physician.
fields.publicSummaryPlaceholder: E.g. « Avoid controlled zone for 6 months »
fields.publicSummaryDescription: Visible to RPO/PCR — operational wording only, no clinical details.

errors.restrictionsRequired: Restrictions description required for this level
errors.reviewRequired: Review date required for temporary unfit

dossier.loadError: Unable to load the medical dossier. Please try again later.
dossier.rbacBlockedBody: You do not have the required permissions to view this medical dossier.
dossier.downloadAttestationHint: Download the annual dose attestation as PDF (GDPR reason required).
dossier.downloadCareerHint: Download the full doses + fitness history (MEDICAL or SELF only — GDPR reason required).
dossier.currentFitnessSubtitle: Last signed card, visible to RPO/PCR and physician.
dossier.noFitness: No signed fitness card for this worker.
dossier.noVisits: No medical visit recorded for this worker.
dossier.linkOverexposureSubtitle: Overexposure cases linked to this worker (Phase 5).
dossier.reasonModalBody: Access to detailed clinical data is recorded nominally (GDPR art.30 + IAEA GSR Part 3). Provide the clinical reason for your consultation.
dossier.reasonPlaceholder: E.g. « Preparing annual periodic visit » / « Post-exposure fitness review »

myMedical.confidentialBanner: Confidential — your personal medical and dosimetric data. Access is logged (GDPR art. 30 + IAEA GSR Part 3).
myMedical.loadError: Unable to load your dossier. Please try again later.
myMedical.unauthenticatedBody: You must be signed in to view your personal dossier.
myMedical.notExposedBody: You are not enrolled in the exposed workers registry of this mine. If you believe this is an error, contact your RPO/PCR.
myMedical.fitnessSubtitle: Last fitness card signed by the occupational physician.
myMedical.noFitness: No signed fitness card to date.
myMedical.detailedRestrictionsNotice: Detailed clinical restrictions are accessible only through the occupational physician. Contact the health service for any question.
myMedical.noVisits: No medical visit recorded to date.
myMedical.cumulSubtitle: Year {{year}} — comparison with regulatory limits ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3.
myMedical.iprcNotice: Limits compliant with ICRP 103 and IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106. Cumulatives are updated automatically with each new measurement.
myMedical.alertsSubtitle: One or more values have triggered a threshold. The RPO/PCR and the occupational physician have been notified.
myMedical.attestationSubtitle: Official document summarizing your dose cumulatives for the current year and the last 5 years.
myMedical.attestationCtaHint: Generates your annual signed PDF dose attestation.
myMedical.attestationModalSubtitle: Personal document summarizing your dose cumulatives for the current year, compliant with GDPR art. 15 access right.
myMedical.attestationSelfReason: Personal access to my own data (GDPR art. 15 — employee direct access right).
myMedical.rgpdFooterBody: In accordance with the GDPR (art. 15 to 22), you have rights of access, rectification, erasure and portability of your personal data. To exercise these rights or for any question, contact your organization's Data Protection Officer (DPO).

dashboard.title: Dosimetry Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Aggregated view of key indicators for ionizing radiation exposure — ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3 compliant. No nominative or medical data is exposed on this dashboard.
dashboard.loadError: Unable to load dosimetry KPIs. Check your permissions or try again.
dashboard.footer: Aggregated data compliant with GDPR art. 30 + ICRP 103 + IAEA GSR Part 3. Snapshots are refreshed daily (nightly job).

kpi.workersTooltip: Total number of exposed workers in the filtered scope. Breakdown by category listed below.
kpi.avgDoseTooltip: Weighted average by worker count per category. Expressed in mSv/year.
kpi.over50Tooltip: Workers whose annual cumulative dose exceeds 50% of the ICRP limit for their category.
kpi.over90Tooltip: Workers at action level — an overexposure case should be considered.
kpi.overexposureTooltip: Click to open the overexposure cases workflow (Phase 5).
kpi.fitnessExpiringTooltip: Click to open the medical surveillance planning (occupational physician).

trend.title: Monthly trends (12 months)
trend.subtitle: Average / median / max annual dose. Ref. lines: 50% / 75% / 100% ICRP limit.

distribution.title: Annual dose distribution
distribution.subtitle: Worker distribution by % of regulatory limit (ICRP 103).

top.title: Top 10 most exposed workers
top.subtitle: Year {{year}} — pseudonymized annual cumulative. Click a row to open the 360 file (RBAC DOSIMETRY_READ_NOMINATIVE).
top.empty: No exposed worker registered for this period.
top.privacyNotice: Aggregated data — no medical data visible here. Worker name and badge number are only visible in the 360 file (permission DOSIMETRY_READ_NOMINATIVE).

multimine.title: Multi-site comparison
multimine.subtitle: KPIs aggregated by mine for the latest snapshot date. Click a card to filter the dashboard.

reports.title: Dosimetry reports and attestations
reports.subtitle: Generate nominative and aggregated reports compliant with ICRP 103 / IAEA GSR Part 3. Every nominative download is logged in the GDPR art. 30 audit register.
reports.rgpdBanner: Individual attestations, career summaries and overexposure reports are nominative documents: a written access reason is mandatory (X-Reason). The download is logged.
reports.loadError: Unable to load required data (workers / cases). Check your permissions or try again.
reports.footerBody: All reports are digitally signed (PAdES). Attestations remain valid as long as no later dose correction invalidates the version. Nominative exports are kept for 30 years in the audit log (IAEA GSR Part 3 §3.106).

attestation.title: Individual dose attestation
attestation.subtitle: Nominative PDF per worker and year — Hp(10), Hp(0.07), Hp(3) cumulatives with ICRP limit comparison.
attestation.modalSubtitle: Official nominative document. The access reason will be transmitted to the backend through the X-Reason header and logged in the GDPR register.

career.title: Career summary
career.subtitle: Full history of doses and medical fitness across the worker's entire career.
career.rbacHint: Required permission · DOSIMETRY_MEDICAL or SELF (strict GDPR)
career.modalSubtitle: Confidential document including aggregated medical data. Strictly reserved to occupational physicians or the worker themselves.

register.title: Annual mine register
register.subtitle: A4 landscape pseudonymized register of exposed workers and their dose cumulatives for the year.

overexposure.title: Overexposure report
overexposure.subtitle: Detailed report of an exceedance case: cause, corrective actions, medical decision, authority declaration.
overexposure.modalSubtitle: Nominative document linking a worker to an exceedance event. The access reason is mandatory and traced.

modal.reasonDescription: Describe in plain text the use of this document (internal audit, judicial request, medical follow-up, etc.). Minimum 10 characters.
modal.reasonPlaceholder: E.g. Annual medical audit — occupational physician Dr. Martin's request for the employee's fitness review.
modal.reasonTooShort: Reason must be at least 10 characters long.
modal.legitimacyLabel: I confirm this request is legitimate and the document will be used only within my professional duties.
modal.rgpdNotice: This download will be logged in the audit register (GDPR art. 30 + IAEA GSR Part 3) with your identifier, timestamp and the reason entered.
modal.errorGeneric: Document generation failed. Please try again.
modal.errorForbidden: Access denied. You do not have the required permission for this document.
modal.errorNotFound: Document not found. Check the parameters and try again.
modal.errorBadRequest: Invalid request. Is the reason properly filled in?

regulatoryExports.title: Regulatory exports ASN / IRSN
regulatoryExports.subtitle: Generation of annual declaration files for the Nuclear Safety Authority, the national regulator and the Radiation Protection Institute. Restricted to PCR / RPO.
regulatoryExports.auditBanner: All institutional exports are logged in bulk (action=REGULATORY_EXPORT) in the audit register. 30-year retention.
regulatoryExports.deniedBody: Regulatory exports are restricted to Radiation Protection Officers (PCR / RPO). Contact your administrator if you need this access.
regulatoryExports.filtersHint: Select the mine and year for which to generate the declaration files.
regulatoryExports.errorGeneric: Export generation failed. Please try again.
regulatoryExports.errorForbidden: Permission denied. This export is restricted to PCR / RPO.
regulatoryExports.errorNotFound: No data available for this scope.
regulatoryExports.footerBody: Generated files comply with the official ASN (XML), national regulator CSV and IRSN incidents schemas. A digital signature is automatically added (PAdES for PDF, XAdES for XML).

xmlAsn.title: Annual XML ASN
xmlAsn.subtitle: Full annual declaration for the Nuclear Safety Authority (official schema).

csvRegulator.title: Regulator CSV
csvRegulator.subtitle: Nominative data for declaration to the ministry and national health authority.

xmlIncidents.title: Annual incidents XML
xmlIncidents.subtitle: All threshold exceedances of the year (open, investigating, closed) in IRSN format.

confirmModal.title: Confirm institutional export
confirmModal.body: This export will be logged in the audit register with your identifier and scope details. Continue?
confirmModal.acknowledge: I confirm I have the authority to generate this institutional export.
